channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 4 Open-Source Security Tools Every Dev Should Know
item.description: Go's standard library is solid. The ecosystem is mature. But none of that protects you from leaked secrets, vulnerable dependencies. Security tooling fills the gap. The good news: the best tools...

item.title: Building a Skills Updater Pipeline for AI Platforms
item.description: I turned 1,870 JSONL files into six new user-level skills for my AI platform in a single session. Here’s how I built a repeatable pipeline for skills-updater. The Problem I had a one-off question:...

item.title: Google shuts down Project Mariner
item.description: Google has pulled the plug on Project Mariner, an experimental feature designed to perform tasks for you across the web, as reported earlier by Wired's Maxwell Zeff. The Project Mariner landing...

item.title: Validating agentic behavior when “correct” isn’t deterministic
item.description: How to build the “Trust Layer” for Github Copilot Coding Agents without brittle scripts or black-box judgements by using dominatory analysis. The post Validating agentic behavior when “correct”...

item.title: How I Conquered the Brimble Task: Insights and Strategies
item.description: Table of Contents Introduction Understanding the Brimble Task Deployment Strategy and Debugging Workflow System Architecture and Core Components Concrete Implementation Details Request–Response...

item.title: Stop editing 500 products: rule-based discounts in WooCommerce
item.description: My wife Lena paints. Oils, acrylics, watercolors. Flowers, landscapes, the occasional cello or violin from her music series. Each painting is its own thing, photographed and listed on her store at...

item.title: Protecting Keycloak Auth with Proof of Work
item.description: I got tired of watching our login endpoint get hammered by bots. Credential stuffing, brute force, the usual nonsense. Rate limiting helps, but it's blunt — one script kiddie from a datacenter and...

item.title: AGEF explained: a portable evidence format for AI agent sessions
item.description: If you ship AI-assisted code in a regulated codebase and somebody asks "show me what the agent did", you have about a week before that question turns into a finding. The data exists somewhere. It...

item.title: Local Deep Research: Run Your Own AI Research Assistant, Fully Private
item.description: If you have ever wished you could throw a complex question at an AI and get back a proper cited report — not a hallucinated paragraph, but something that actually searched the web, read papers,...

item.title: Seven third-party script patterns we triage before touching CSS
item.description: We still hear the same opener in client calls: "Could this be our CSS?" Sometimes yes. Often no. On plenty of sites, the first real bottleneck is not your h1 style or a spacing token. It is...

item.title: Building SwiftDeploy: A Declarative Infrastructure CLI with Observability and Policy Enforcement
item.description: What Is This Project? SwiftDeploy is a command-line tool that automatically sets up and manages web application deployments. Instead of manually configuring Docker containers, Nginx, and...

item.title: Mirai-Based xlabs_v1 Botnet Exploits ADB to Hijack IoT Devices for DDoS Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have exposed a new Mirai-derived botnet that self-identifies as xlabs_v1 and targets internet-exposed devices running Android Debug Bridge (ADB) to enlist them in a...

item.title: How David Sacks crashed and burned in the White House
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter exclusively for Verge subscribers about tech, politics, and Washington intrigue. (It's basically House of Cards, but for nerds.) Not a subscriber yet?...

item.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
The latest Tile Pro is down to $25 — its best price of the year
item.description: If you’re struggling to find a Mother’s Day gift under $30, location trackers are a good investment, helping mom keep tabs on keys, bags, luggage, and other valuables. The latest Tile Pro is one...

item.title: Anthropic Gets in Bed With SpaceX as the AI Race Turns Weird
item.description: In an unexpected turn, the two companies signed a deal for Anthropic to use computing resources from Elon Musk’s xAI.

item.title: How Elon Musk left OpenAI, according to Greg Brockman
item.description: Cutthroat negotiations between startup founders are rarely shared so publicly, especially when a company becomes as world-changing as OpenAI.

item.title: What we’re hearing about the new homepage
item.description: We’ve been reading your feedback on the new homepage. All of it. The kind and encouraging comments, the brutal ones, the “actually this rules,” the “please undo this immediately,” the bug reports,...

item.title: Using AI for Just 10 Minutes Might Make You Lazy and Dumb, Study Shows
item.description: New research suggests that reliance on AI assistants can have a negative impact on people’s ability to think and problem solve.

item.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
item.description: Mira Murati, OpenAI's former CTO, has testified under oath that CEO Sam Altman lied to her about the safety standards for a new AI model. In a video deposition shown during the ongoing Musk v....

item.title: The 44 best last-minute Mother’s Day gift ideas for 2026
item.description: Whether it's managing a busy home or looking out for everyone around them, moms spend a lot of time every day caring for others. This Sunday, Mother's Day, is an opportunity to return the favor,...

item.title: SpaceX may spend up to $119B on ‘Terafab’ chip factory in Texas
item.description: The project would be a "multi-phase, next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility," according to the proposal.

item.title: Here’s what Microsoft is offering long-serving employees to voluntarily retire
item.description: Microsoft revealed last month that it's planning to offer long-serving employees in the US the ability to voluntarily retire. While the terms of the buyout were supposed to be announced to...

item.title: DeepSeek could hit $45B valuation from its first investment round
item.description: The Chinese AI lab came to prominence in early 2025 after launching a large language model that trained on a fraction of the compute power and at a fraction of the cost of the big U.S. models like...

item.title: I Am Begging AI Companies to Stop Naming Features After Human Processes
item.description: Anthropic announced “dreaming” for AI agents to sort through “memories” at its developer conference. Can we not?

item.title: Native Instruments Komplete 26 adds weird new synths and experimental piano sounds
item.description: The latest version of Native Instruments' Komplete music production bundle is here with 62 new additions, including the wonderfully weird Absynth 6. Komplete 26 comes in several flavors, starting...

item.title: The best beauty tech you can still grab in time for Mother’s Day
item.description: If you’re looking to indulge mom this Mother’s Day, a gift that makes her feel a little more taken care of is a good place to start. Fortunately, if you’re looking for something to make her feel...

item.title: This slim ice cream maker could fit in my already crowded kitchen
item.description: For the past few months, I've defiantly walked past the Ninja Creami at Costco as I think about our air fryer, toaster oven, breadmaker, rice cooker, and stand mixer already vying for limited...

item.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search

item.title: Google updates AI search to include quotes from Reddit and other sources
item.description: While citing web forums and discussion boards can help users find answers to more niche queries, this design choice could also prove chaotic.
